content.problemStatement:
You are given a large number NNN. You need to print the number N+1N+1N+1.

Note: The number is very large and it will not fit in standard integer data type. You have to take the input as String and then manipulate the digits to convert it to N+1N + 1N+1.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.





Output Format

For each test case, print a single line string - the number N+1N+1N+1.
constraints: The sum of lengths of all N over the whole input does not exceed 400000.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single non‑negative integer N given as a decimal string (without leading zeros, except the number 0 itself).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing the decimal representation of N+1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each number is increased by one using decimal addition with carry propagation.
0 becomes 1, 9 becomes 10, and the large number increments at the last digit.
All‑9 number expands length, while 129 carries only to the tens place.

solution.approach: We cannot store N in built‑in integer types, so we treat it as a string. Starting from the least significant digit we add one, propagate the carry while the digit becomes 10, and stop when the carry becomes zero. If we finish the whole string with a remaining carry, we prepend '1'. This runs in O(L) time per test case where L is the number of digits, and uses O(1) extra space besides the input string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of digits) — each digit is visited at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space (output string reuses the input buffer).
